message:
chore: rolling sync of concurrent agent changes

Capturing latest changes from concurrent agent development.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1rz0.2.3","title":"Resize Scheduler: Anytime-Valid Decision Rule (e-process)","description":"## Task
Define anytime-valid decision rules (e-process) for when to act under uncertainty in resize scheduling.

## Scope
- Specify e-process thresholds and stopping rules.
- Integrate with change-point detection and controller outputs.

## Tests / Validation
- Property tests: decisions remain valid under adversarial sequences.
- Simulation logs include evidence values and stop decisions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Decision rules are explainable and documented.
- Scheduler can justify actions in logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:12.705030592Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T01:05:36.360202331Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T01:05:36.360184157Z","close_reason":"Documented e-process decision rule, log-space update, thresholds, and logging fields in resize-scheduler spec","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["math","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":336,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Anytime‑Valid Decision Rule (e‑process)

E‑value Definition
- Construct e_t such that E[e_t] ≤ 1 under null (no regime shift).
- Example: likelihood ratio between storm vs steady models.

Decision Rule
- Trigger storm mode if e_t ≥ 1/α (α = tolerated false‑positive rate).
- Safe under optional stopping (resize stream can end anytime).

Computation
- e_t = Π (p(x_t|storm) / p(x_t|steady)) with clipping to avoid underflow.
- Use log‑space accumulation for numerical stability.

Guarantee
- False alarm probability ≤ α regardless of stopping time.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:59:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rz0.2.4","title":"Invariant Proof Sketches + Failure Mode Ledger","description":"Proof sketches for invariants, plus explicit failure mode and mitigation ledger.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Invariant Proof Sketches + Failure Mode Ledger
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:12.888199761Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T06:31:06.144427416Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T06:31:06.144400986Z","close_reason":"Added proof sketches + failure mode ledger to resize-scheduler spec","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":337,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.2.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Invariant Proof Sketches + Failure Mode Ledger

Core Invariants
1. Continuous reflow: UI reflects latest size within SLA bound.
2. Atomic present: each frame is internally consistent (no partial clears).
3. No placeholder: real UI always rendered during resize.
4. No ghosting: shrinking clears stale regions deterministically.

Proof Sketches (outline)
- Atomic present: single buffered write + sync output ⇒ no interleaving.
- No ghosting: full clear of removed regions + reset scroll region ensures old cells removed.
- Continuous reflow: coalescer emits latest size each tick; scheduler bound ensures max delay.

Failure Mode Ledger
- Mux without sync output: fallback to overlay redraw + full clear.
- Extremely rapid resize: throttle via PI controller but never stalls input.
- Terminal caps mis-detection: conservative fallback and diagnostic log.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:59:36Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-32my","title":"Demo Showcase: Layout Composer","description":"## Background

Layout is the core promise of a TUI framework. FrankenTUI has dedicated layout solvers (flex + grid), and this screen exists to make that power obvious:
- interactive split panes
- live constraints
- deterministic, stable tie-breaking

The demo should feel like a miniature "layout IDE".

## UX / Core Interactions

- A tree of split panes (horizontal/vertical) with resizable dividers.
- Keyboard and mouse resizing.
- A live "constraint inspector" panel showing:
  - the constraints per node
  - computed sizes
  - which constraints are active/binding

Suggested keybindings:
- Arrow keys: move focus between panes
- Ctrl+Arrow: resize focused split
- M: toggle mouse resizing handles
- Tab: cycle inspector modes (raw constraints / computed sizes / slack)

## Architecture Notes

- Model split layout as a small layout graph:
  - nodes: panes
  - edges: splits with ratios/constraints
- Use ftui-layout as the solver; avoid writing a separate solver.
- Render with widgets + a minimal overlay for resize handles.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Resize interactions are smooth and deterministic.
- Constraint responses are monotone and stable:
  - increasing a pane's min width never increases a different pane below its min
  - tie-breaking is deterministic across runs
- Works in both alt-screen and inline mode.

Additionally (project-wide standards):
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Layout invariants: feasibility, stable tie-breaking, monotone constraint response; add metamorphic/property tests

## Alien Artifact Upgrades (Concrete)

- Sensitivity analysis:
  - show how a small change in a constraint affects computed sizes (a "derivative" / slack view)
- Counterexample-driven tests:
  - generate random constraint graphs and assert invariants; when a failure occurs, minimize the counterexample and store as a regression fixture

## Extreme Optimization

- Solver performance budgets:
  - define O(n) / O(n log n) expectations for typical graphs
  - add microbench for interactive resize loops","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:31:47.047413517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:28:58.419236730Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","layout","ux"],"comments":[{"id":399,"issue_id":"bd-32my","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Scope
- Interactive split panes with resize handles.
- Display live constraints and resulting sizes.

Testing
- Unit tests for constraint updates and layout stability.
- PTY E2E tests with verbose logs for resizing interactions.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:31:47Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-32my.5","title":"Layout Composer — Diagnostic Logging + Telemetry Hooks","description":"Add rich diagnostic logging and optional telemetry hooks for Layout Composer to aid debugging and demos.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Layout Composer — Diagnostic Logging + Telemetry Hooks
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
